top_categories.workout_context: 76 miles on great Ocean Road coastal and Otway Ranges climbing burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
65ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Amy's Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
When someone attacks on great Ocean Road coastal and Otway Ranges climbing, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Amy's Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Amy's Gran Fondo's great Ocean Road coastal and Otway Ranges climbing forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 76 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on great Ocean Road coastal and Otway Ranges climbing does to your power file on race day.
At Amy's Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Base fitness for Amy's Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Amy's Gran Fondo, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.
The aerobic base that lets you ride great Ocean Road coastal and Otway Ranges climbing at tempo for 76 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Amy's Gran Fondo’s 76 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 76 miles of great ocean road coastal and otway ranges climbing in Lorne, Great Ocean Road, Victoria, Australia.
